title: Q2 Quiz 2 Draft: Titles, Transitions, and Final Export

questions.text: What is a title used for in an edit?
choices: Readable text such as a title card, name label, or lower third.
A raw camera file.
A no-school calendar note.
A timeline backup folder.
questions.correctAnswer: Readable text such as a title card, name label, or lower third.
questions.explanation: Titles communicate text information inside the video.

questions.text: Which panel is commonly used to adjust title text, position, and selected effect settings?
choices: Inspector
Project Manager
Render Queue
Media Storage
questions.explanation: The Inspector exposes controls for selected clips, titles, transitions, and effects.

choices: A point in time that stores a value so a property can change over time.
The final exported file.
A folder inside the Media Pool.
The first day of the grading period.
questions.correctAnswer: A point in time that stores a value so a property can change over time.
questions.explanation: Keyframes make motion or property changes happen over time.

questions.text: When should a transition be used?
choices: When it supports the edit and does not distract from the story.
Between every single clip no matter what.
Only before importing footage.
Only after the final file is exported.
questions.correctAnswer: When it supports the edit and does not distract from the story.
questions.explanation: Transitions should serve clarity, pacing, or tone instead of becoming decoration.

questions.text: What makes a lower third or title card classroom-ready?
choices: It is readable, spelled correctly, timed well, and supports the viewer.
It is as small as possible.
It covers the entire edit for the whole video.
It replaces the need to organize clips.
questions.correctAnswer: It is readable, spelled correctly, timed well, and supports the viewer.
questions.explanation: Student titles should improve communication without hiding the video.

questions.text: Which page is used to render or export the finished timeline?
choices: Deliver
Media
Edit
Project Manager
questions.explanation: The Deliver page is used to render the timeline into a finished video file.

choices: A list of export jobs waiting to render.
A list of raw clips not imported.
A folder of quiz answers.
A set of A/B class periods.
questions.correctAnswer: A list of export jobs waiting to render.
questions.explanation: Resolve adds export jobs to the render queue before rendering.

questions.text: Why should the file name and save location be checked before rendering?
choices: So the exported file can be found and submitted correctly.
So the project deletes unused clips.
So the timeline automatically rewrites itself.
So the grading period changes.
questions.correctAnswer: So the exported file can be found and submitted correctly.
questions.explanation: Naming and location checks prevent lost exports and confusing submissions.

choices: A method used to encode and compress video or audio data.
A title animation preset only.
A class join code.
A type of bin folder.
questions.correctAnswer: A method used to encode and compress video or audio data.
questions.explanation: Codecs such as H.264 and H.265 affect how a video file is encoded.

questions.text: Which export setting is commonly used for a web-friendly class submission?
choices: H.264 in an MP4 container.
A missing file path.
A project file with no rendered video.
A timeline with no title or final check.
questions.explanation: H.264 MP4 files are widely playable and practical for class review.

questions.text: Why should an editor watch the final timeline before exporting?
choices: To catch gaps, unreadable titles, timing problems, or other mistakes.
To skip the render queue.
To delete the project manager.
To avoid saving the file.
questions.correctAnswer: To catch gaps, unreadable titles, timing problems, or other mistakes.
questions.explanation: A final playback check catches issues before a file is submitted.

questions.text: What should a simple motion effect do in a school edit?
choices: Guide attention or polish the communication without distracting.
Move randomly for the whole video.
Replace the need for a rough cut.
Hide all required evidence.
questions.correctAnswer: Guide attention or polish the communication without distracting.
questions.explanation: Motion should be intentional and support the edit.

questions.text: Identify the term: a visual change used between two clips, such as a dissolve or wipe.
questions.explanation: Transitions control how one clip changes into another.

questions.text: Identify the term: a list of export jobs waiting to be rendered.
questions.explanation: The render queue stores export jobs before rendering begins.

questions.text: Name two final quality checks an editor should make before exporting.
questions.correctAnswer: A strong answer can include title readability, spelling, timing, timeline gaps, final playback, file name, save location, or export settings.
questions.explanation: This checks whether students can name real pre-export checks that affect the viewer or submission workflow.
